title: cerastium glabratum subsp. glaberrimum
intro: Cerastium glabratum subsp. glaberrimum, also known as the woolly mouse-ear chickweed, is a plant species in the carnation family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving skin conditions, and supporting immune function. Cerastium glabratum subsp. glaberrimum can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in salves and creams for topical application.
